issues.description: The loop was changed from `0..n` to `0..=n`, making it iterate N+1 times instead of N. When `i == n`, both `params[i]` and `vertex[i]` will panic with an index-out-of-bounds error because `params` and `vertex` (cloned from `initial`) both have length N. The original `0..n` was correct: the simplex needs N+1 vertices total (1 initial + N perturbations). With `0..=n` it would attempt to create N+2 vertices but will crash before completing.
issues.suggested_fix: Revert the loop range back to `for i in 0..n`. The original range was correct for building an N-dimensional simplex.

issues.description: The new `validate_simplex` function is defined but never called anywhere. It provides a useful invariant check, but without being wired into the code path (e.g., as a debug_assert after `build_simplex`), it provides no runtime benefit.
issues.suggested_fix: Add `debug_assert!(validate_simplex(&simplex, n))` immediately after the `build_simplex` call in `solve_core`, or invoke it as a post-condition inside `build_simplex` itself before returning.

issues.description: The new `scale` parameter has no bounds validation. Negative or zero values would produce degenerate simplices (collapsed vertices), and very large values could push all vertices to the same clamped bound. The existing test `simplex_has_n_plus_1_vertices` would need updating for the new signature.
issues.suggested_fix: Add a `debug_assert!(scale > 0.0 && scale <= 1.0)` at the top of `build_simplex`, or clamp the scale to a valid range.

opus_bug_hunter.summary: Critical index-out-of-bounds bug: the loop range was changed from `0..n` to `0..=n`, which will cause a panic on every invocation. When `i == n`, both `params[n]` and `vertex[n]` are out of bounds since both slices have length `n`. The original `0..n` range was correct — an N-dimensional simplex requires exactly N+1 vertices (1 initial + N perturbations). The new `validate_simplex` function, which would catch this exact invariant violation, is never called. The added `scale` parameter also lacks bounds validation.

issues.description: Changing the loop from `0..n` to `0..=n` causes an out-of-bounds panic. When `i == n`, both `params[i]` and `vertex[i]` are out of bounds — `params` has `n` elements (indices 0..n-1) and `vertex` is cloned from `initial` which also has `n` elements. This will panic at runtime on every call to build_simplex.
issues.suggested_fix: Revert the loop range back to `0..n`. The original range is correct: a simplex in N dimensions requires N+1 vertices (1 initial + N perturbations), not N+2.

issues.description: The function signature adds a `scale` parameter but the call site at line 568 (`build_simplex(initial, &problem.auto_params)`) and all test call sites (lines 2171, 2190, 2215) are not updated. This will fail to compile.
issues.suggested_fix: Update the call site at line 568 to pass a scale value (e.g. `build_simplex(initial, &problem.auto_params, 0.1)`) and update all test call sites similarly. The default behavior should match the previous hardcoded `0.1`.

issues.location: crates/reify-constraints/src/solver.rs (new `validate_simplex` function at end of diff)
issues.description: `validate_simplex` is defined but never called anywhere. It also cannot validate the output of the broken `build_simplex` since that now produces N+2 vertices while `validate_simplex` expects N+1. If this is intended for debug assertions, it should be used (e.g. `debug_assert!(validate_simplex(&simplex, n))` after building).
issues.suggested_fix: Either add a call to `validate_simplex` as a debug assertion inside `build_simplex` or at the call site, or remove it to avoid dead code. Consider making it a `#[cfg(debug_assertions)]` assertion inside `build_simplex` itself.

issues.description: The docstring still says 'each offset by a fraction of the parameter range' but the fraction is now caller-controlled via `scale`. The doc should document the new parameter and its expected range.
issues.suggested_fix: Update the docstring to document the `scale` parameter, e.g.: '`scale` — fraction of the parameter range used for initial perturbation (e.g. 0.1 for 10%).'

opus_design_critic.summary: This diff contains two blocking bugs. First, the loop range change from `0..n` to `0..=n` will cause an index-out-of-bounds panic on every invocation — both `params[i]` and `vertex[i]` are accessed at index `n` which is past the end of both slices, and it produces N+2 vertices instead of the required N+1. Second, the new `scale` parameter is added to the function signature but no call sites are updated, so the code will not compile. The parameterization of `scale` is a good design improvement (replacing a magic constant), and `validate_simplex` is a reasonable helper, but neither can work until the loop range and call sites are fixed.
